responseMessageContents.text: The user is asking for a comprehensive audit of the app and landing page capabilities across roughly 100 modules, so I need to systematically explore the codebase structure—backend APIs, services, mobile screens, landing page, and related platforms. I'll start by listing the key directories to get a sense of the module breakdown and file organization.
I'll audit the actual codebase live — backend API modules, app screens, landing page, and native apps — then break it down into the module list.

responseMessageContents.text: I've got the audit data ready, so I'll compile the full report now organized by domain with around 100 modules. I'll be straightforward about what's implemented natively versus through Expo or backend services, and I'll number everything clearly for easy reference.

Full end-to-end audit, measured live from the codebase just now (not memory). Counted: **111 backend API modules, 273 backend services, 254 app screens, 63-section landing page**. Here is the 100-module breakdown:

## A. Landing Page — ivxholding.com (8 modules)
1. Hero + live deal showcase (pulls real deals, error-state fallback)
2. Lead funnel — multi-step form (first/last/email/phone, progress bar, live member count)
3. Invest-from-landing flow — deal picker, amount input, min/frequency display
4. Landing auth — signup/login overlay with password + error handling
5. How-it-works + credibility sections
6. Support chat widget (`landing-support-chat.js` → public chat AI)
7. Cookie banner, disclosures, App-coming-soon + Android badge
8. SEO — robots.txt, sitemap.xml, capture/analytics hooks

## B. Public App — Auth & Onboarding (8)
9. Signup / 10. Login / 11. Password reset / 12. 2FA authenticator
13. KYC verification / 14. Personal & tax info / 15. Member + enterprise registration / 16. Waitlist

## C. Investing Core (12)
17. Home dashboard / 18. Market browse / 19. Property detail (`property/[id]`)
20. Buy shares / 21. Sell shares / 22. Gift shares / 23. Resale marketplace
24. Portfolio / 25. Auto-reinvest / 26. Copy investing / 27. Smart investing + compare / 28. Debt acquisition, land-partner & submit-property flows

## D. Wallet & Finance (6)
29. Wallet / 30. Statements / 31. Tax documents / 32. VIP tiers / 33. IPX earn / 34. Fees & investor profits (admin-side ledger)

## E. Growth & Social (6)
35. Referrals / 36. Viral growth / 37. Broker, agent & influencer applications
38. QR code + business card / 39. Share content / 40. Investor pitch, prospectus & video presentation

## F. Communications (7)
41. Chat hub + chat rooms / 42. Public AI support chat (vision-capable, Supabase-backed)
43. Email compose/inbox/detail / 44. SMS compose, dashboard, history, reports
45. Notifications + settings / 46. Broadcast engine / 47. Gmail provider integration

## G. Legal & Trust (5)
48. Legal center / 49. Trust center / 50. Contract & JV agreement generator / 51. Title review + property documents / 52. Security settings

## H. Admin Suite (18)
53. Admin dashboard + control tower / 54. Members & team mgmt / 55. Properties mgmt / 56. Transactions / 57. Applications review / 58. Audit log + registration audit / 59. Email engine + accounts mgmt / 60. Marketing, retargeting, engagement / 61. Landing control + landing analytics + submissions / 62. Lead & visitor intelligence / 63. Lender directory/search/sync / 64. Title companies / 65. JV deals + land partners / 66. AI outreach + AI video / 67. Social command + growth / 68. System monitor + system map + sync diagnostics / 69. Data recovery, trash bin, image backup / 70. Feature control, API keys, staff activity

## I. IVX Owner AI Command Center (22)
71. Owner login/signup/access gate / 72. Owner AI chat (streaming) + owner AI jobs/log
73. IVX Senior Developer runtime + worker (build/deploy tools) / 74. Autonomous core/cycle/mode/OS/scale
75. Multi-agent + parallel agents + role agents / 76. Capital command center / 77. Capital network + pipeline + outreach / 78. Investor CRM + import + dedup / 79. Investor/buyer discovery / 80. Deal matching + deal tracking / 81. Lead scoring + master lead list + lead audit / 82. Campaigns + outreach engine / 83. CTO dashboard + engineering intelligence / 84. Executive layer + action loop / 85. Daily report + business impact + metrics / 86. Incidents + diagnostics + production diagnostics / 87. Deploy control + Render deploy/diagnostic + GitHub sync / 88. Runtime variables + owner variables + security box / 89. Access requests + user permissions + invite staff/roles / 90. Unified/operational/IA memory / 91. Independence + Rork-independence status / 92. Proof ledger, worker proof, durability proof, live coding stream

## J. Backend Infrastructure (6)
93. Hono server (4,022 LOC) on Render — live at api.ivxholding.com, commit-verified
94. Supabase store + export + inspection / 95. Route53 DNS mgmt / 96. Scheduler + night ops / 97. Cloudflare Worker (`functions/`) live at ivx26-backend.rork.app / 98. Test suite (version, commit-match, chat durability, worker proofs)

## K. Native iOS — the honest part (2)
99. **`ios-ivx` (Ivx)** — owner auth only: login, auth gate, session, tab shell (~8 Swift files)
100. **`ios` (IVX26)** — the app you're previewing: **still the empty placeholder, 0 modules**

**Bottom line:** modules 1–98 are real and live via the React Native app + Node backend. Native Swift coverage is ~1 module out of 100. Want me to start porting the top modules (auth → home → market → buy shares) into the native IVX26 app?
